evals.prompt: I need to create an Azure AI agent in Python that can analyze CSV files and generate bar charts. It should use code interpreter and I want to stream the response to the user in real time. I have my PROJECT_ENDPOINT and MODEL_DEPLOYMENT_NAME set as env vars already.
evals.expected_output: Complete Python code that: (1) creates an AIProjectClient with DefaultAzureCredential, (2) uploads the CSV file and sets up CodeInterpreterTool with the file ID, (3) creates an agent with the code interpreter, (4) creates a thread and message, (5) uses runs.stream() with a streaming handler or iteration to print tokens in real time, (6) retrieves and saves any generated image files after the run.
assertions: Code imports AIProjectClient from azure.ai.projects and DefaultAzureCredential from azure.identity
Code uses CodeInterpreterTool with file_ids parameter
Code uses agents_client.runs.stream() or equivalent streaming method (not just runs.create with polling)
Code shows how to print streaming delta text (MessageDeltaChunk)
Code includes file retrieval for generated images/files after the run
Code uses os.environ for PROJECT_ENDPOINT and MODEL_DEPLOYMENT_NAME

evals.prompt: How do I connect an Azure AI agent to an MCP server? I have a hosted MCP server at https://my-tools.internal.company.com/sse and I want the agent to use it, but I need manual approval before each tool call since it's accessing sensitive data.
evals.expected_output: Code showing McpTool setup with the server URL, agent creation with the MCP tool, and a manual approval loop or RunHandler that intercepts requires_action status, checks for RequiredMcpToolCall, and submits ToolApproval objects.
assertions: Code creates McpTool with server_label and server_url parameters
Code creates an agent with mcp_tool.definitions as tools
Code shows the manual approval pattern: either a polling loop checking for requires_action + SubmitToolApprovalAction, or a RunHandler subclass with submit_mcp_tool_approval method
Code imports SubmitToolApprovalAction, RequiredMcpToolCall, and ToolApproval
Code does NOT call set_approval_mode('never') since the user wants manual approval

evals.prompt: I'm using the agent-framework library with AzureAIAgentsProvider and I want my agent to return structured data as a Pydantic model. The agent should extract product information (name, price, category, in_stock boolean) from user messages. Show me how to set this up with async code.
evals.expected_output: Async Python code using AzureAIAgentsProvider with a Pydantic model for structured output, showing async context manager usage, the Pydantic model definition with the required fields, and how to pass the response format when running the agent.
assertions: Code defines a Pydantic BaseModel class with name (str), price (float), category (str), and in_stock (bool) fields
Code uses async with syntax for both the credential and provider
Code calls provider.create_agent() with appropriate instructions
Code passes the Pydantic model as response_format in agent.run() OR uses ResponseFormatJsonSchema with the model's JSON schema
